rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO MARIN COUNTY** CONS. OF DONALD PAUL LERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) $300 was paid from this conservatorship, but should have been paid by a special needs trust created by Ctee's father, for tax return services. Petitioner requests that this court order that reimbursement be made by her as trustee of the SNT and that the reimbursement be deemed not to affect public benefits eligibility. 2) Conservator transferred funds to establish a CalABLE account. Petitioner requests retroactive authority to establish the account. 3) $7,941 was spent on a trip to Disneyland. 4) Conservator reimbursed herself for some costs without receipts, some unknown costs, and some costs based upon information and belief. Petitioner requests that no surcharge be assessed, but if they are, that they only be assessed against the "unknown" transactions. 5) Attorney requests $26,257 in fees, which is roughly about what is alleged to be in this estate as of 4/4/22. 6) Conservatee has been residing in San Rafael. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MAURICIO ANTONIO VARELA CARIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: * ERROR *
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) The additional relief added in Amend Petition should have been filed in a sperate petition. LRSF 14.47.M instructs such a substituted judgment petition to be a stand-alone petition. This petition seeks multiple requests. (2) Petition does not allege creating the irrevocable trust will be exempted under the sole benefit rule. Paragraph 14 only address payments from the SNT. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD E. ANDERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/1/23. (No earlier availability.) 1. Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] 2. Notice of Hearing did not list all of the exhibits filed separately and on a later date from the petition (not "attached" as alleged in the petition). 3. The statutory fee calculation did not reflect the losses ($5,250.00) and must not include receipts that were refunds for amounts paid out of the estate or returned from the estate. 4. Please file a copy of the closing statement to confirm the costs of sale of the real property. 5. The administrator's expenses may need supporting documentation. The list should show the date, the payee, the full description, and the amount. "Storage and Moving", for example, is not sufficient. 6. Renella Anderson post-deceased the decedent on 8/22/21 and, at the time the petition to determine entitlement was filed, no estate had reportedly been opened yet. No Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits appear to have been filed either. Distribution to Renella Anderson cannot be made "c/o Schynell Strong" without some authority. 7. The attorney's fee declaration does not appear to fully meet the requirements of CRC 7.702. 8. After these issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required, as the administrator hired his own daughter and paid her without any Court authority. At the very least, amounts he paid to her must be subtracted from his statutory compensation, as she provided services the administrator should have. (It is unnecessary to recite all the contents of I&As that were superseded by later amended I&As; only the final one is the one on which the accounting should be based. Notice should not be served on people who have been determined to no longer be interested parties. The administrator does not need to give notice to himself. Only the financial account statements for the end of the accounting period need to be filed.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] It appears that a second amendment will need to be filed. The only proper form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed, not a Civil form of proof of service without the Notice of Hearing attached. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KALVIN M. MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/26/23. (No earlier available date.) No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] No proof of service has been filed. There are three other heirs entitled to notice. Do the administrator's siblings have any issue? If they disclaimed their right, their interests passes to their issue. Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] If the statutory fee/commission is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] Deadline for delivering the proposed order and courtesy copies to the assigned examiner is 4/14/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (NB: Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed. A Fidelity investment account had just cash, no securities?)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS RUTHERFORD JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for failure to adddress the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 11/07/2022): 1) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. 2) Item 1 of the petition: The newspaper name must be specified. 3) Item 2d: i) A declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate and describe such liabilities and claims. ii) A minimum bond ($20,000) is required for a proposed personal representative who does not reside in California, regardless of bond waivers. See LRSF 14.16(F). 4) Item 3g(2)(a) must be checked. 5) Item 3g(2)(c) must be checked, with the petitioner's relationship to the decedent specified. 6) Item 3h(3) must be checked. 7) Item 8: i) The age of each person must be specified. ii) The names and dates of death of the decedent's parents must be provided. iii) Are the petitioner, Lesley Jackson, and Carolyn Jackson the only siblings the decedent ever had? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLAN MCKILLOP
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 3/18/2024. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed by 3/4/24. Appearance will be required on 3/18/24 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY JANE WILLIAMSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing per written order modifying that the minimum bond of $20,000 is required for a non CA resident. Status of closing the estate is set for 4/15/2024.
